result: If I had the power to change the world in one profound way, I would ensure that every person has access to meaningful empathy—both receiving it and offering it. When empathy is woven into daily life, it reshapes how we communicate, govern, innovate, and care for one another. It pushes us to look beyond our own needs, to build systems that are just, to resolve conflicts before they harden into violence, and to recognize the shared humanity that spans cultures, borders, and generations. From that one shift, I believe we'd see ripples of progress in education, health, environmental stewardship, and peace—because understanding gives us the wisdom to act with both heart and mind.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
